title: Joby Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results
text: Joby Aviation reported Q3 2025 financial results with continuing progress on FAA certification and manufacturing expansion. For full year 2025, JOBY Aviation reported a loss of US$1.13 per share, and revenue reached US$53.4 million. Q1 2025 loss per share was US$0.11, with a net loss of US$82.4 million. Q2 2025 loss per share was US$0.41 with a net loss of US$324.7 million. Q3 2025 net loss was $401 million. The company had strong cash reserves with $1.4 billion in cash and short-term investments as of Q3 2025. The company received an additional net $1.2 billion in February 2026. The company's test aircraft completed more than 850 flights in 2025—a 260 percent increase over 2024. The company is progressing through final FAA certification stages with expected TIA testing in 2026. Manufacturing capacity continues to expand at facilities in California and Ohio. The company maintains focus on achieving multiple operational milestones including continued FAA certification progress and expanded Department of Defense testing.
